description_long: This record player features a rectangular wooden base with a rich, reddish-brown finish, giving it a classic and elegant appearance. The large, circular black turntable platter dominates the left side, with a subtle concentric ring design at its center. On the right, a metallic tonearm with a black finish extends over the platter, connected to a pivoting mechanism and a visible counterweight. There are several rectangular black control panels or buttons embedded into the wooden surface, symmetrically placed on the front left and right corners. The overall design is sleek and minimalistic, with no visible dust cover, exposing the mechanical components. The materials and craftsmanship suggest a blend of vintage aesthetics and modern functionality.
description: A vintage-style record player with a wooden base, black platter, and metallic tonearm, featuring embedded control panels and a sleek, open design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular platter, tonearm resting to the right, and two rectangular control panels on the front corners.
description_view_1: Right-side view highlighting the length of the tonearm, the pivot mechanism, and the platter's edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the tonearm assembly, the platter, and the absence of a dust cover.
description_view_3: Left-side view focusing on the platter's surface, the tonearm extending across, and the control panel on the left.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, large, and circular, occupying a significant portion of the left side of the record player. It is black with a subtle concentric ring design at the center, has a smooth matte finish, and appears to be made of plastic or metal with a possible rubber mat for grip. The platter is slightly raised above the wooden base.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic with a black finish, and extends from the right side of the base over the platter. It is straight, of medium length, and features a visible pivot mechanism and counterweight at the rear. The tonearm is currently resting in its holder, not positioned over the platter.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present, attached to the end of the tonearm. It is small, pointed, and appears to be made of metal and plastic, designed to make contact with a record on the platter.
attribute_control_knobs: There are two rectangular black control panels or buttons embedded into the wooden surface, one on the front left and one on the front right. They are flat, elongated, and likely made of plastic, but no visible labels or markings are present.
